mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above 5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite 3-month PAYEMS decline, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling robust consumer demand and corporate earnings expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 705692 million dollars as of 2023-11-01
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns is < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS surprises do not produce statistically significant positive SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating both supply-side weakness and labor market softening. This dual deterioration signals stagflationary pressure and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or supply shock regime
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of INDPRO decline and UNRATE rise does not produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million annualized units while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, indicating synchronized weakness in both residential investment and overall economic growth. This dual contraction signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1.2M annualized AND GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when both HOUST < 1.2M and GDPC1 growth < 2%, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, indicating that monetary policy has successfully tightened financial conditions without triggering a demand collapse. This 'soft landing' signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as recession risk diminishes.
regime_hint: soft landing scenario with disinflation and elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of soft-landing instances, or correlation between inflation deceleration and positive SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises above 4.0% while nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth turns negative, signaling a sharp deterioration in labor market conditions. This dual labor-market weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors anticipate earnings compression from reduced consumer spending.
regime_hint: labor market contraction or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 4.0% and PAYEMS declines, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows at an annualized rate above 3% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling robust manufacturing activity and supply-side expansion. This supply-side strength supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprice earnings upward.
regime_hint: expansion regime with strong manufacturing momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day SPX returns is < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO surprises do not produce statistically significant positive SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).
